kit: motion-primitives (motion-primitives.com) — open-source React animation primitives by ibelick, built on the "motion" package (framer-motion successor)
installation: Three options per component. 1) Dedicated CLI: `npx motion-primitives@latest add <name>` (e.g. `npx motion-primitives@latest add text-effect`). 2) shadcn CLI registry: `npx shadcn@latest add "https://motion-primitives.com/c/<name>.json"` (requires a components.json in the project; registry items are type registry:ui and land in your configured ui dir). 3) Manual copy-paste from each doc page's Installation section (source also at github.com/ibelick/motion-primitives under components/core/). Prerequisites from /docs/installation: Tailwind CSS, `npm install motion`, `npm install lucide-react`, and a `lib/utils.ts` cn() helper (clsx + tailwind-merge).
dependencies: motion (^11.12.0 in repo; v12 also works)
lucide-react (^0.400.0, icons used in examples and dialog close buttons)
tailwindVersion: Kit targets Tailwind CSS v4 (repo uses tailwindcss ^4.0.0 with @tailwindcss/postcss) — matches your Astro + Tailwind v4 stack with zero config; components use plain utility classes + cn(), no tailwind plugin required (tailwindcss-animate in the repo is for the docs site only).

components.name: Text Effect
components.blogUse: Homepage hero title entrance (per-word blur/fade preset) and post-page H1 reveal; supports per char/word/line, presets, custom variants, delays, exit animations

components.name: Text Loop
components.blogUse: Rotating word in hero subtitle (e.g. 'writing about X / Y / Z')

components.name: Text Morph
components.blogUse: Code-block copy button 'Copy' -> 'Copied' morph; theme-switcher or header label swaps

components.name: Text Scramble
components.blogUse: 404 page headline scramble-in; still reads minimal/Swiss

components.name: Text Shimmer
components.blogUse: Subtle status text: newsletter 'Subscribing...' state, loading indicators; tasteful in dark mode

components.name: Text Roll
components.blogUse: Header nav link hover roll micro-interaction

components.name: Text Shimmer Wave
components.blogUse: Wavy shimmer headline — too flashy for a minimal Swiss blog

components.name: Spinning Text
components.blogUse: Circular rotating text badge — decorative, off-style for minimal design

components.name: In View
components.blogUse: Scroll-reveal wrapper for post list items, about-page sections, footer CTA; viewport-triggered fade/blur-in

components.name: Animated Group
components.blogUse: Staggered entrance of the post card grid on homepage and tags page (presets: fade, blur-slide, scale)

components.name: Animated Background
components.blogUse: Shared-layout hover/active pill behind header nav links, tag filter pills, and segmented theme switcher

components.name: Scroll Progress
components.blogUse: Reading-progress bar fixed at top of post pages (spring-smoothed useScroll)

components.name: Progressive Blur
components.blogUse: Gradient edge blur under sticky header / above footer so content fades out as it scrolls beneath

components.name: Spotlight
components.blogUse: Mouse-follow spotlight on post cards — excellent dark-first card hover treatment

components.name: Disclosure
components.blogUse: Collapsible table of contents on post pages (esp. mobile) and expandable post-card excerpts

components.name: Morphing Dialog
components.blogUse: Image lightbox for prose images (click to zoom, shared-layout morph) and post-card -> preview expansion; ships with useClickOutside hook

components.name: Magnetic
components.blogUse: Subtle magnetic hover on footer/header social icons and the newsletter submit button

components.name: Tilt
components.blogUse: Gentle 3D tilt on post cards (pure CSS transform, no three.js) — keep rotation factor low for Swiss style

components.name: Glow Effect
components.blogUse: Soft glow behind newsletter CTA button/card in dark mode — use sparingly as garnish

components.name: Border Trail
components.blogUse: Animated border light on newsletter input/card focus state

components.name: Accordion
components.blogUse: FAQ section on about page; overlaps with Disclosure so pick one primary

components.name: Transition Panel
components.blogUse: Animated switching between tag-filtered post lists or about-page tabs

components.name: Animated Number
components.blogUse: Count-up stats on about page (posts written, years coding) with in-view trigger

components.name: Sliding Number
components.blogUse: Odometer-style digits: footer clock or view counters; needs react-use-measure

components.name: Text Loop
components.blogUse: Duplicate entry guard — see earlier Text Loop entry

components.name: Dialog
components.blogUse: Animated modal for site search (Cmd+K) or newsletter signup

components.name: Morphing Popover
components.blogUse: Newsletter CTA button morphing into an inline email form popover

components.name: Toolbar Dynamic
components.blogUse: Floating share/actions toolbar on post pages (share, copy link, back to top)

components.name: Carousel
components.blogUse: Featured-posts carousel on homepage — a static grid is more Swiss; use only if featured content exists

components.name: Toolbar Expandable
components.blogUse: Expandable icon toolbar — redundant with Toolbar Dynamic for a blog

components.name: Dock
components.blogUse: macOS-style magnifying dock — too playful for a minimal Swiss blog header/footer

components.name: Cursor
components.blogUse: Custom animated cursor — hurts readability on a text-focused blog

components.name: Infinite Slider
components.blogUse: Marquee of logos/tags — visual noise for a minimal blog

components.name: Image Comparison
components.blogUse: Before/after slider — only useful inside specific post content, not blog chrome

notes: 1) Every core component starts with 'use client' — in Astro each usage must be a React island (client:visible for below-fold reveals like InView/AnimatedGroup, client:load for ScrollProgress/header nav, client:idle for garnish like Magnetic/Spotlight). No module-top-level window access observed, so SSR render + hydrate is safe. 2) React 19: the kit's own site pins react ^18 (Next 14), but components use no React-18-only APIs and motion v11.12+/v12 officially supports React 19; shadcn CLI install works under React 19 (may prompt --force/--legacy-peer-deps on npm). 3) No three.js or other heavy deps anywhere — the only extra runtime deps are motion, plus react-use-measure for sliding-number; morphing-dialog/morphing-popover also install a hooks/useClickOutside.tsx file (registry:hook). 4) shadcn registry path is /c/<name>.json (not /r/): npx shadcn@latest add "https://motion-primitives.com/c/text-effect.json"; full registry index at https://motion-primitives.com/c/registry.json. 5) The website itself sits behind a Vercel security checkpoint that can 403/429 automated fetches — source of truth is github.com/ibelick/motion-primitives (app/docs/*/page.mdx for docs, components/core/*.tsx for code). 6) One duplicate 'Text Loop' row appears in the list above due to an editing slip — treat the first entry (priority: must) as canonical; the kit has exactly 33 components. 7) TextEffect exit animations need the component kept mounted (AnimatePresence semantics) — for Astro page transitions prefer entrance-only. 8) Respect prefers-reduced-motion yourself; primitives do not gate animations automatically.
